summary.headline: ADM reports third quarter 2022 earnings per share of $1.83, $1.86 on an adjusted basis.
summary.narrative_summary: Archer-Daniels-Midland Company (ADM) reported third quarter 2022 financial results with earnings per share of $1.83, and adjusted earnings per share of $1.86. The company's segment operating profit was $1.559 billion, with strong performance across Ag Services and Oilseeds, Carbohydrate Solutions, and Nutrition segments. The results reflect resilient global demand, improved margins, and effective risk management, while also noting some headwinds such as lower volumes in certain areas and demand fulfillment challenges.

drivers: short crops in South America supporting U.S. exports
improved volumes and margins in North American origination
better margins in global ocean freight
resilient global demand for both meal and oil
positive impacts from an insurance settlement
North American soy crush margins benefiting from renewable diesel demand
concerns: impacts from idled facilities in Ukraine and Paraguay

drivers: steady global demand for sweeteners and starches
improved year-over-year results in Starches and Sweeteners subsegment
continued robust demand for corn oil
concerns: Vantage Corn Processors results were substantially lower
ethanol margins pressured by lower domestic demand and elevated corn costs

drivers: continued strong demand growth in EMEA
concerns: adverse currency translation effects in EMEA
demand fulfillment challenges in North America
lower demand in APAC due to lockdowns in China
Health Wellness was lower versus the prior year

drivers: higher short-term interest rates driving improved earnings in ADM Investor Services
concerns: increased claim settlements in captive insurance

extraction_notes: Numbers from Exhibit 99.1; cover sheet only had Item references. Some financial figures were not explicitly stated in the text but were implied or referenced in the press release.
